User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the DineFlow Global platform, including customers, restaurant administrators, and super administrators. Links to Firebase Authentication for credentials.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for login and notifications.

name.description: The full name of the user.

role.description: The role of the user within the platform (e.g., 'SuperAdmin', 'RestaurantAdmin', 'Customer').

restaurantId.description: Reference to the Restaurant this user is an admin for. This field is only applicable and required for users with the 'RestaurantAdmin' role. (Relationship: Restaurant 1:1 User for RestaurantAdmin role)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was last updated.

Restaurant.title: Restaurant
Restaurant.description: Represents a restaurant managed on the DineFlow Global platform, including its details, localization, and SEO settings.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Restaurant entity.

name.description: The official name of the restaurant.

description.description: A brief description of the restaurant.

address.description: The physical address of the restaurant.

contactEmail.description: The primary contact email for the restaurant.

contactPhone.description: The primary contact phone number for the restaurant.

baseLanguage.description: The primary language used for the restaurant's operations and content (e.g., 'en', 'fr').

baseCurrency.description: The primary currency used for pricing and transactions (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR').

seoMetaTags.description: JSON string or similar representation for dynamic meta tags to enhance local SEO.

seoSchemaMarkup.description: JSON string or similar representation for schema.org 'LocalBusiness' markup to enhance local SEO.

localizedUrlSlugs.description: An array of localized URL slugs for the restaurant (e.g., '/uk/london/pizzeria').

adminUserId.description: Reference to the User who is the primary administrator for this restaurant. (Relationship: Restaurant 1:1 User)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the restaurant was onboarded to the platform.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the restaurant details were last updated.

Menu.title: Menu
Menu.description: Represents a menu offered by a restaurant, containing a collection of menu items.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Menu entity.

restaurantId.description: Reference to the Restaurant this menu belongs to. (Relationship: Restaurant 1:N Menu)

name.description: The name of the menu (e.g., 'Lunch Menu', 'Dinner Menu', 'Drinks').

description.description: A brief description of the menu.

isActive.description: Indicates if the menu is currently active and visible to customers.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the menu was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the menu was last updated.

MenuItem.title: MenuItem
MenuItem.description: Represents an individual food or drink item available on a restaurant's menu.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MenuItem entity.

menuId.description: Reference to the Menu this item belongs to. (Relationship: Menu 1:N MenuItem)

name.description: The name of the menu item.

description.description: A detailed description of the menu item, including ingredients.

price.description: The price of the menu item.

currency.description: The currency of the menu item's price (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR'). Defaults to restaurant's base currency.

inventoryLevel.description: The current stock level for this menu item, for real-time inventory management.

category.description: The category the item belongs to (e.g., 'Appetizer', 'Main Course', 'Dessert').

imageUrl.description: URL to an image of the menu item.

isAvailable.description: Indicates if the menu item is currently available for order.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the menu item was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the menu item was last updated.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents a customer's order placed at a specific restaurant.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

restaurantId.description: Reference to the Restaurant where the order was placed. (Relationship: Restaurant 1:N Order)

customerId.description: Reference to the User who placed the order. (Relationship: User 1:N Order)

orderItems.description: An array of items included in the order, capturing their state at the time of order.

totalAmount.description: The total amount of the order, including taxes and fees.

currency.description: The currency of the total order amount.

status.description: The current status of the order (e.g., 'Pending', 'Confirmed', 'Preparing', 'ReadyForPickup', 'Delivered', 'Cancelled').

deliveryAddress.description: The delivery address for the order, if applicable.

paymentTransactionId.description: Reference to the PaymentTransaction associated with this order. (Relationship: Order 1:1 PaymentTransaction)

orderedAt.description: Timestamp when the order was placed.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the order status was last updated.

PaymentTransaction.title: PaymentTransaction
PaymentTransaction.description: Records details of a payment transaction processed via Stripe Connect for an order.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PaymentTransaction entity.

orderId.description: Reference to the Order associated with this payment. (Relationship: Order 1:1 PaymentTransaction)

stripeChargeId.description: The unique ID provided by Stripe for this charge.

amount.description: The amount processed in the transaction.

currency.description: The currency of the transaction amount.

status.description: The status of the payment transaction (e.g., 'succeeded', 'failed', 'pending', 'refunded').

processedAt.description: Timestamp when the payment transaction was processed.

customerId.description: Reference to the User (customer) who made the payment. (Relationship: User 1:N PaymentTransaction)

Subscription.title: Subscription
Subscription.description: Manages the subscription status and plan details for each restaurant on the platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Subscription entity.

restaurantId.description: Reference to the Restaurant associated with this subscription. (Relationship: Restaurant 1:1 Subscription)

startDate.description: The date when the subscription became active.

endDate.description: The date when the subscription is set to expire.

status.description: The current status of the subscription (e.g., 'Active', 'Expired', 'Cancelled', 'Trial').

stripeSubscriptionId.description: The unique ID provided by Stripe for this subscription.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the subscription record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the subscription record was last updated.

definition.description: Stores user profiles, roles, and associated restaurant IDs (for Restaurant Admins). The document ID (`userId`) matches Firebase Authentication's UID. Includes 'role' field and, for Restaurant Admins, 'restaurantId' for direct authorization checks.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: A marker collection to quickly identify Super Administrator users. The existence of a document with the user's UID in this collection signifies Super Admin privileges, enabling efficient DBAC checks without custom claims.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Super Administrator user, corresponding to Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Top-level collection for all restaurant-specific data. Each document represents a restaurant and includes 'adminUserId' for ownership and authorization. Subcollections below this path will inherit the 'restaurantId' context.

params.name: restaurantId
params.description: The unique identifier of the restaurant.

definition.description: Stores menus belonging to a specific restaurant. Includes denormalized 'restaurantId' and 'adminUserId' (from the parent Restaurant document) for authorization independence and QAP compliance.

params.name: restaurantId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent restaurant.

params.name: menuId
params.description: The unique identifier of the menu.

definition.description: Stores individual menu items belonging to a specific menu within a restaurant. Includes denormalized 'restaurantId', 'menuId', and 'adminUserId' for authorization independence and QAP compliance.

params.name: restaurantId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent restaurant.

params.name: menuId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent menu.

params.name: menuItemId
params.description: The unique identifier of the menu item.

definition.description: Stores customer orders placed at a specific restaurant. Includes denormalized 'restaurantId', 'adminUserId' (from the parent Restaurant document), and 'customerId' for robust authorization checks by both restaurant admins and customers, ensuring QAP compliance.

params.name: restaurantId
params.description: The unique identifier of the restaurant where the order was placed.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the order.

definition.description: Records payment transactions for orders placed at a restaurant. Includes denormalized 'restaurantId', 'adminUserId' (from the parent Restaurant document), 'orderId', and 'customerId' for authorization independence and QAP compliance.

params.name: restaurantId
params.description: The unique identifier of the restaurant associated with the payment.

params.name: paymentTransactionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the payment transaction.

definition.description: Manages subscription details for a specific restaurant. The 'subscriptionId' often matches the 'restaurantId' due to a 1:1 relationship. Includes denormalized 'restaurantId' and 'adminUserId' for authorization independence and QAP compliance.

params.name: restaurantId
params.description: The unique identifier of the restaurant associated with the subscription.

params.name: subscriptionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the subscription, ideally matching the restaurantId.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure prioritizes security, scalability, and debuggability, strictly adhering to the core design principles and mandates. The multi-tenant nature of the 'DineFlow Global' platform is addressed by employing a 'Shared Database, Isolated Data' model, with `/restaurants/{restaurantId}` as the primary segregation point for all restaurant-specific data.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):** To eliminate the need for `get()` calls in security rules and enable atomic operations, authorization context is extensively denormalized. For all documents residing under a specific restaurant (e.g., `Menu`, `MenuItem`, `Order`, `PaymentTransaction`, `Subscription`), the `adminUserId` (which identifies the restaurant's primary administrator) is denormalized directly into each document. This means a rule for a `menu` item can directly check `resource.data.adminUserId == request.auth.uid` without needing to fetch the parent `Restaurant` document, thereby making rules robust and efficient. Furthermore, customer-specific orders and payment transactions also include `customerId` for direct ownership checks.

**DBAC (No Custom Claims):** User roles are stored explicitly in the database. The `/users/{userId}` collection holds each user's profile, including their `role` (e.g., 'Customer', 'RestaurantAdmin', 'SuperAdmin'). For `RestaurantAdmin` users, their associated `restaurantId` is also stored directly within their user document. To facilitate high-performance, clear authorization for Super Administrators, a separate top-level collection, `/superAdmins/{userId}`, is used. The mere existence of a document with `request.auth.uid` as its ID in this collection grants Super Admin privileges, adhering to the 'Existence over Content' principle for global roles. This avoids `get()` calls on the `users` collection to determine if a user is a Super Admin for every privileged action.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** Secure `list` operations are supported by structural segregation and denormalization. For instance, a Restaurant Admin can only list menus, menu items, orders, or subscriptions from `/restaurants/{restaurantId}/...` where `restaurantId` matches the ID they manage (verified by the denormalized `adminUserId`). Similarly, a customer can list their own orders from `/restaurants/{restaurantId}/orders/{orderId}` by querying `customerId == request.auth.uid`. The path structure itself, combined with denormalized fields, ensures that queries inherently return only authorized data, rather than requiring security rules to filter results post-query (which is not how Firestore rules work).

**Structural Segregation:** Data with different security postures or ownership models are placed in separate collections. `/users/{userId}` for individual user profiles, `/superAdmins/{userId}` for Super Admin markers, and `/restaurants/{restaurantId}` for all restaurant-specific, multi-tenant data. Subcollections within `/restaurants/{restaurantId}` (e.g., `menus`, `orders`) further segregate data while inheriting the `restaurantId` context.

**Data Clarity:** Path wildcards are descriptive (`{restaurantId}`, `{userId}`). Entity schemas are used consistently. The `status` field in entities like `Order` and `Subscription` explicitly models the state.
